title: Decider context-load extraction — split decider-curmudgeon.md so Step 2a (expansion integration) loads only when Priority 4 fires

headline_recommendation.selected: Extract Step 2a (Integrate Completed Expansions, lines 100-364 of decider-curmudgeon.md, 265 lines) into a new conditional reference file. Load it ONLY at Priority 4 (`monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json` has complete/revised+!integrated items), which empirically fires on ~13% of decider runs. Keep Step 2 (digest), Step 2b (yeet), Mode Selection, Cycle 3+ Reviews, and Issue Closure in the always-loaded decider-curmudgeon.md.
headline_recommendation.rationale: decider-curmudgeon.md (490L) is loaded under Priorities 3, 3b, and 4. Of its content, 265 lines (Step 2a) are ONLY needed when Priority 4 fires. Empirical measurement across the last 23 decider runs (2026-05-11 through 2026-05-16T05:22Z, sampled via daily-report-*.json `expansions_integrated`/`m2_auto_close`/`m3_carryover` fields): 3/23 runs (~13%) touched expansion integration. The other 20/23 (~87%) ran Priorities 3 and 3b but did NOT need Step 2a's 265 lines. Average savings per run = 265 × 0.87 = ~230L, exceeding the directive's 150-200L target. Frequency assumption in directive (~70% empty queue) was orthogonal — the better predictor is Priority-4-fires rate, which gives a tighter conditional and higher savings.
headline_recommendation.in_one_line: Move Step 2a (expansion integration, 265L) to monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md, loaded conditionally at decider.md Priority 4. Update 4 cross-references. No behavior change.

executive_summary.problem_one_line: Decider total context load is +25.8% vs 2026-05-09 baseline (FIND-2026-05-16-07 MAJOR). PROP-037 application drove decider-curmudgeon.md from 306L → 490L. Each addition was operator-approved and closes a real bug, but the cumulative load crosses the MAJOR threshold and material-but-not-crisis at ~$20/year/agent of pure input cost (compounds across continued PROP adoption).
executive_summary.fix_one_line: Conditional-load extraction. The 265-line Step 2a (expansion integration, including queue push at Step 7, M2 EXP-tied auto-close at Step 8b, and M3 carry-over enforcement at Step 8c) is only needed when Priority 4 fires (~13% of runs). Move it to a new sibling reference; load conditionally. Phase 1 is decider-only per operator constraint.

tinker_top_line_answers_to_directive_que.Q1_how_does_decider_detect_priority_queu: Decider does NOT condition on priority-queue depth. The honest trigger is 'Priority 4 fires' (expansion-tracker.json has complete/revised+!integrated items). This is already a dispatcher check at decider.md line 149: `t.items.filter(i=>(i.status==='complete'||i.status==='revised')&&!i.integrated)`. When that filter returns ≥1, Priority 4 fires and the new conditional file is read. When it returns 0, Priority 4 does not fire and the new file is NOT loaded. This is the same pattern decider already uses for Priority 1 (new WIN onboarding loads decider-intake.md). Reframing the directive's 'priority-queue non-empty' as 'Priority 4 fires' improves accuracy: priority-queue depth and Priority-4-firing are different signals — measured data (last 23 runs) shows queue non-empty ~83% but Priority 4 fires only ~13%. The latter is the right conditional.
tinker_top_line_answers_to_directive_que.Q2_always_needed_vs_conditional_boundary: Conservative split: ALL of Step 2a (lines 100-364) goes to the conditional file. This includes Step 2a Steps 1-9: no-op check, category-proposal-writeup routing, progressive-disclosure validation, integration mechanics, queue push, issue closure, M2 EXP-tied auto-close, M3 carry-over enforcement, and out-of-scope-issue-filing rule. All 9 sub-steps only fire when an expansion is being integrated; none are needed for Priority 3 (digest) or Priority 3b (BAU triage). Cross-references from decider.md line 14 (Step 4b), state-verification.md (Step 2), and decider-reporting.md (Step 4b) point at content that STAYS in decider-curmudgeon.md — no breakage. Cross-references from decider.md line 139, routing-matrix.md lines 8/21 point at Step 8c (which moves) — these will be updated to the new file path. Cycle 3+ Reviews (lines 433-480, ~48L) is also conditional in principle (only fires when defense_survives≥3) but stays in the always-loaded file in Phase 1 because (a) it's small, (b) it's referenced from decider-curmudgeon.md Step 2a so the cross-file load pattern would couple the two more tightly than the savings justify, and (c) operator-preference conservative-when-in-doubt.
tinker_top_line_answers_to_directive_que.Q3_extends_to_other_agent_splits: No. PROP-034 (analyst-baby split) and PROP-038 (curmudgeon-verify split) are AGENT splits — different cron, different model, different review_class routing. PROP-040 is a CONTEXT-FILE split — same agent, same model, different priority-conditional reference loading. Different mechanism. Phase 2 candidates in analyst.md (Mode 0 new-WIN-onboarding ~40L, Mode 3 defense-neutralization ~50L) and curmudgeon.md (Cycle 3+ advocate-mode procedure ~50-80L estimated) are surfaced below but explicitly deferred to a future PROP. tinker.md is already well-extracted (mode procedures live in tinker-*.md sub-references); no useful further split. Phase 1 is decider-only per operator constraint.

files_read: build.js OWNERSHIP block (auto-classifies *.md under monitor/prompts/ as git-owned — no explicit entry needed)
monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH (dynamic rule covers monitor/prompts/**/*.md — no explicit entry needed)
CLAUDE.md File Ownership table (no explicit entry needed for the new file — dynamic rule applies)
23 daily-report-*.json files spanning 2026-05-11 through 2026-05-16T05-22 (Priority-4-fires frequency measurement)

directive_reference_check.tinker_filename_concern: The name 'pq-mechanics' is slightly misleading because the extracted content is mostly expansion-integration mechanics (Steps 1-6 = integration; Step 7 = queue push; Steps 8-9 = closure mechanics; Step 8b = M2; Step 8c = M3). Only Step 7 (~25L of the 265L) is literally queue-push mechanics. A more accurate name would be 'decider-curmudgeon-integration.md' or 'decider-expansion-integration.md'. RECOMMENDATION: proceed with the operator's specified 'decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md' for Phase 1 (avoid re-litigating directive specs); if operator prefers the more accurate name on review, rename is a 1-line edit to the conditional-load instruction. Surfaced as a counter-argument below.
directive_reference_check.directive_says_priority_queue_non_empty_: Tinker's FIND-2026-05-16-07 recommendation phrased the trigger as 'priority-queue is non-empty'. On closer measurement, the better trigger is 'Priority 4 fires' (expansion-tracker has work). 87% Priority-4-not-fires vs 70% queue-empty — the file-load conditional is tighter and more honest. No directive constraint violated — directive intent is conditional loading; specific signal is tinker's design call.
directive_reference_check.directive_top5_cycle_lines_check: Directive claims decider.md 520→769L (+249L, +47.9%), decider-curmudgeon.md 306→490L (+184L, +60.1%). As of 2026-05-16T06:30Z: decider.md 769L (matches), decider-curmudgeon.md 490L (matches). Baseline counts from 2026-05-09 not re-verified directly but inherited as accurate per tinker's prior measurement.

priority_4_fires_frequency.method: Count daily-report-*.json files in monitor/decisions/ with expansions_integrated || m2_auto_close || m3_carryover fields present
priority_4_fires_frequency.comment: Tinker's original FIND-07 estimate said ~70% queue-empty. Priority-4-fires is a different signal: queue depth lingers because curmudgeon hasn't reviewed pushed items yet, while Priority-4-fires depends on analyst having completed an expansion AND decider not having already integrated it. The latter is rarer.

projected_savings.cost_savings_per_year_per_agent: 230L × 3 tokens/line × $5/M input × 6 runs/day × 365 days = ~$7.55/year (significantly less than the $20/year FIND-07 estimate because FIND-07 measured raw growth, not amortized-by-frequency savings). Material but not crisis-level. Cost savings is the secondary benefit; the primary benefit is attention/focus on Priority-3-only and Priority-3b-only runs.
non_cost_benefits: Reduced attention dilution on Priority-3 and Priority-3b runs (87% of runs no longer carry 265L of integration mechanics)
Clearer separation of concerns: decider-curmudgeon.md becomes about review processing + yeet + closure; pq-mechanics.md becomes about integration mechanics
Establishes precedent for future conditional extractions (PROP-040 is the first context-file split following the agent-splits of PROP-034/038)

phase_1_design.scope: decider-curmudgeon.md split only. No analyst/curmudgeon/tinker edits in Phase 1.
phase_1_files_changed: monitor/prompts/decider.md (3 cross-reference updates + 1 conditional-load instruction addition)
monitor/prompts/reference/tinker-cost-engineering.md (1 sum-line update: add the new file to the decider context-load measurement)
CLAUDE.md (1 line addition to File Map for the new reference file — optional, dynamic-rule covers ownership)
phase_1_files_NOT_changed: build.js OWNERSHIP — *.md under monitor/prompts/ auto-classifies as git-owned via dynamic rule (build.js lines 147-150). No edit.
monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH — dynamic rule covers monitor/prompts/**/*.md (workspace-sync.md line 95-96 case glob). No edit.
monitor/prompts/decider.md line 14 (Step 4b cross-reference) — Step 4b STAYS in decider-curmudgeon.md. No edit.
monitor/prompts/reference/state-verification.md line 83 (Step 2 cross-reference) — Step 2 STAYS in decider-curmudgeon.md. No edit.

verbatim_line_ranges_to_move.boundary_check_top: Line 99 is the last line of Step 4b: '**RS-a is already handled by the gate above (Step 1–4): you re-grep the cited content before creating a new issue. Step 4b is the parallel rule for RE-DECLARATION.**'. Line 100 is the Step 2a header: '## Step 2a: Integrate Completed Expansions (EVERY run, do FIRST)'. Clean boundary.
verbatim_line_ranges_to_move.boundary_check_bottom: Line 364 is the last line of Step 2a Step 9 (out-of-scope-to-issues rule). Line 365 is the Step 2b header: '## Step 2b: Yeet Scan (EVERY run)'. Clean boundary.
verbatim_line_ranges_to_move.verbatim_assertion: Move byte-for-byte. No content changes during the move. The conditional-load instruction in decider.md is the ONLY new prose authored in Phase 1; the moved content is verbatim from the source.
verbatim_line_ranges_to_move.preamble_to_prepend_in_new_file: The new file gets a brief preamble (3-5 lines) added BEFORE the moved Step 2a content. The preamble describes: (a) when this file should be loaded (Priority 4 fires), (b) what it contains (expansion integration mechanics, including queue push at Step 7, M2 EXP-tied auto-close at Step 8b, M3 carry-over enforcement at Step 8c), (c) cross-reference to the always-loaded decider-curmudgeon.md for Step 2 (digest), Step 2b (yeet), Mode Selection, Cycle 3+ Reviews, and Issue Closure. Preamble adds ~6 lines beyond the moved content.

phase_1_design.preamble_content_for_new_file:
# Decider: Expansion Integration Mechanics (Priority-4-conditional)

**This file is loaded ONLY when decider.md Priority 4 fires** — i.e., `monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json` has items with `(status==='complete' || status==='revised') && !integrated`. When Priority 4 does NOT fire, this file is NOT read.

Contains: Step 2a expansion integration (Steps 1-9 — no-op handling, category-proposal-writeup routing, progressive-disclosure validation, integration mechanics, queue push at Step 7, issue closure at Step 8, M2 EXP-tied auto-close at Step 8b, M3 carry-over enforcement at Step 8c, out-of-scope-issue-filing rule at Step 9).

For Step 2 (digest processing), Step 4/4b (staleness gates), Step 2b (yeet scan), Mode Selection (batch processing), Cycle 3+ Reviews, and Issue Closure, see the always-loaded `decider-curmudgeon.md`.

conditional_load_instruction_for_decider.current_text:
**Priority 4 — Completed Expansions**
```bash
node -e "const t=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json','utf8'));const c=t.items.filter(i=>(i.status==='complete'||i.status==='revised')&&!i.integrated);console.log(c.length?'EXPANSIONS: '+c.length+' ready to integrate':'NO PENDING EXPANSIONS')"
```
Trigger: Completed expansions not yet integrated into sections.json/wins.json.
→ Read `monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon.md`, execute Step 2a.
conditional_load_instruction_for_decider.replacement_text:
**Priority 4 — Completed Expansions**
```bash
node -e "const t=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json','utf8'));const c=t.items.filter(i=>(i.status==='complete'||i.status==='revised')&&!i.integrated);console.log(c.length?'EXPANSIONS: '+c.length+' ready to integrate':'NO PENDING EXPANSIONS')"
```
Trigger: Completed expansions not yet integrated into sections.json/wins.json.
→ **IF this priority fires** (the bash check above prints `EXPANSIONS: N ready to integrate`), read `monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md` for Step 2a integration mechanics (no-op handling, category-proposal-writeup routing, progressive-disclosure validation, integration mechanics, queue push at Step 7, issue closure at Step 8, M2 EXP-tied auto-close at Step 8b, M3 carry-over enforcement at Step 8c, out-of-scope-issue-filing rule at Step 9). Then execute Step 2a from that file. **DO NOT load this file when Priority 4 does NOT fire** — it is 265L of integration mechanics not needed for Priority 3 (digest processing) or Priority 3b (BAU triage), both of which continue to use `decider-curmudgeon.md` alone.
conditional_load_instruction_for_decider.rationale_for_explicit_DO_NOT_load: The directive specifies the conditional-load instruction must be 'explicit and easy to forget-proof (visible at the top of the related step, not buried in a reference)'. Both the positive (IF fires, read X) and negative (DO NOT load when not firing) sides are stated. The bash check itself is the gate.

decider_md_line_139.current: Same enforcement pattern as M3 carry-over (decider-curmudgeon.md Step 8c self-test).
decider_md_line_139.replacement: Same enforcement pattern as M3 carry-over (decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md Step 8c self-test, loaded conditionally at Priority 4).

decider_md_line_260_comment.replacement: // Self-heal — same archive-aware pattern as decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md EXP allocator.
decider_md_line_260_comment.note: This is a code comment inside decider.md M1 sweep bash. Comment-only; no behavior.

routing_matrix_md_line_8.current: 3. **M3 carry-over** — `monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon.md` Step 8c, per-curmudgeon-review, scope = `unresolved_prior_cycle[]` entries. Ledger `closed_by_mechanism: 'M3'`.
routing_matrix_md_line_8.replacement: 3. **M3 carry-over** — `monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md` Step 8c, per-curmudgeon-review, scope = `unresolved_prior_cycle[]` entries. Ledger `closed_by_mechanism: 'M3'`.

tinker_cost_engineering_md_line_235.rationale: Tinker's context-load measurement must include the new conditional file in the per-run total, even though it's not always loaded. This is the worst-case (Priority-4-fires) load — what tinker should track. A separate measurement of 'amortized load given Priority-4-fires rate' would be a future optimization but the simple max-load measurement is appropriate for trend monitoring.

no_behavior_regression_signals: Queue items popping correctly (priority-queue.json depth changes track curmudgeon review production within ±1 item)
PROP-009 invariants intact (popped_by_queue_id stamps appear on review files; shadow log accumulates only expected patterns)
EXP integration succeeds end-to-end on at least 2 Priority-4-fires runs (tracker item transitions from status='complete'/'revised' to integrated=true with archive append)
M2 EXP-tied auto-close fires on at least 1 EXP integration with M2 candidates (closure-ledger.jsonl appends `closed_by_mechanism: 'M2'` entries)

M3 carry-over enforcement fires on at least 1 review with `unresolved_prior_cycle[]` entries (closure-ledger.jsonl appends `closed_by_mechanism: 'M3'` entries with `action_taken` in {patch, wontfix, escalate, route-to-curmudgeon, route-to-analyst})
No tinker run reports 'STALE' or 'CONTEXT-LOAD-REGRESSION' findings tied to decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md
No curmudgeon review of decider-self-applied patches shows quality regression vs pre-split baseline (sample 5 patches across the 7-day window)

loc_savings_validation.method: On 5 decider runs randomly sampled across the 7-day window, measure: (a) Priority 4 fires bash check result, (b) which reference files the decider's prompt chain actually loaded. Empirical avg savings should be ≥ 200L per run (within ±25% of the 230L projection).
loc_savings_validation.acceptance: ≥150L avg savings empirically (i.e., directive's lower bound). Below 150L → triage to determine if Priority 4 fires more often than measured (possibly burndown-mode artifact) and consider deferring rollout.

hard_rollback: Decider misses an EXP integration that the un-split version would have caught (regression in expansion-tracker → wins.json/sections.json data flow)
M2 or M3 ledger entries stop appearing on integration runs (mechanism silently broken)
Cross-reference dangling — routing-matrix.md or decider-reporting.md cites a file path that doesn't resolve
Operator-judged quality regression on integrated content (e.g., progressive-disclosure validation skipped, queue push omitted)
soft_rollback: Empirical LOC savings <100L (target was 150-200L; severe miss suggests the conditional doesn't fire as expected)
Repeated decider runs where Priority 4 fires but the conditional file is NOT loaded (LLM forgets the read instruction — signal for stricter wording or different mechanism)
phase_1_rollback_criteria.rollback_procedure: git revert the PROP-040 commit. The split is mechanical (text relocation + reference insertion); revert restores byte-identical pre-split state. Estimated revert time: 5 minutes.

phase_2_candidates_NOT_in_phase_1.note: Per directive task_2 + operator constraint, Phase 2 candidates are surfaced but NOT applied in Phase 1. Phase 2 is conditional on Phase 1 holding 7 green days. Each Phase 2 item gets its own PROP — they are not bundled.

extraction_candidates.section: Mode 0 — New WIN Onboarding (analyst.md lines ~85-120, est. ~35L)
extraction_candidates.trigger: monitor/analyst/new-wins/ has files OR poller flagged unmatched WIN headings
extraction_candidates.fires_rate_estimate: rare — only when poller's new-WIN detection finds something. Last 14 days: 3 fires (~10% of analyst runs)

extraction_candidates.section: Mode 3 — Defense Neutralization (analyst.md lines ~200-260 est., needs detailed read)

analyst_md_candidates.phase_2_total_potential: ~80L per analyst run if both extracted

extraction_candidates.section: Cycle 3+ advocate-mode procedure (curmudgeon.md within Per-Run Procedure, est. ~50-80L — needs detailed read for exact boundaries)
extraction_candidates.trigger: Reviewing a WIN whose tracker.json shows current cycle ≥3 with defense-survives potential
extraction_candidates.fires_rate_estimate: rare — Cycle 3+ reviews are a small fraction of total reviews, est. <10%

tinker_md_candidates.note: Tinker is already well-extracted — Mode 1/2/3/4 procedures live in tinker-pipeline-health.md, tinker-infrastructure.md, tinker-cost-engineering.md, tinker-proposals-and-fixes.md respectively. The dispatcher itself (tinker.md) is necessarily always-loaded. No useful further split.

phase_2_candidates_NOT_in_phase_1.rationale_for_phase_2_deferral: Phase 1 establishes the conditional-load pattern with a single, well-understood example. Phase 2 should validate the pattern doesn't introduce attention-fragmentation in agents (where the LLM forgets to load a conditional file because it's now multiple decisions deep). Phase 2 is gated on Phase 1's 7-green-days validation; if Phase 1 shows even ONE 'forgot to load' regression, Phase 2 should not proceed — instead, the conditional-load mechanism needs a stricter design (e.g., bash-driven preamble that forces the LLM to read the file, not just an English instruction).

counter_arguments.operator_directive_explicitly_requires_s: Per directive task_4 + operator preference 'Think about the other side of the argument too' (CLAUDE.md user preferences). Each counter-arg below is real, not strawmanned.

args.argument: Conditional loading is fragile — the agent must remember to read the conditional file. Forgotten reads create silent regressions: decider sees Priority 4 fires, integrates the expansion, but never reads the M2/M3 carry-over logic and silently skips closure mechanics.
args.tinker_rebuttal: Real risk, partially-mitigated. Mitigations: (a) the conditional-load instruction is at the TOP of Priority 4's dispatcher block, visible immediately when Priority 4 fires; (b) the IF/DO-NOT framing makes the instruction explicit on both sides; (c) Phase 1's 7-day window is the validation gate — if the LLM forgets the read even ONCE in the validation window, Phase 1 fails and we abort. Residual risk: the LLM might read the conditional file BUT miss a Step 8b/8c sub-procedure embedded in it (this isn't a read-failure, it's a within-file attention failure). The unsplit baseline had the same risk — splitting doesn't introduce it, but it doesn't fix it either. ACCEPTED RESIDUAL RISK: validated by Phase 1 exit criteria measuring M2/M3 ledger appearance on integration runs.

args.argument: $7.55/year/agent is rounding error vs operator-time spent designing/applying/reviewing this split. Tinker's earlier estimate of $20/year/agent was based on raw decider.md+decider-curmudgeon.md growth; the amortized-by-frequency savings is much smaller (~$7.55/year). The bill of materials doesn't justify the engineering effort.
args.tinker_rebuttal: Cost is NOT the primary justification — context-load also affects model attention, focus, and reasoning. The cumulative-growth signal here is structural: every approved PROP adds capability; without proactive extraction discipline, the cumulative effect crosses MAJOR (>25%) and eventually becomes a real cost (compounds across 10 agents and continued PROP adoption). Phase 1 establishes the extraction practice as a counter-balance to additive PROPs. The $7.55/year figure is downstream evidence, not the driver. ALSO: operator-time-for-application is ~30 min Phase 1 (3 prompt files + 2 cross-references + 1 sum-line); the design work is this PROP. The marginal cost of the split itself is small.

args.argument: The directive's 'priority-queue non-empty' trigger framing is more intuitive than tinker's 'Priority 4 fires' framing. The directive author may push back on the reframing as deviation from spec.
args.tinker_rebuttal: The reframing is the right call empirically. Priority-queue depth (which lingers because curmudgeon hasn't reviewed yet) does NOT correlate with decider needing the integration mechanics — decider only needs them when it's actively integrating. Measured data: queue non-empty in ~83% of runs, Priority-4-fires in ~13% of runs. Conditional on queue-depth would extract content for only 17% of runs (savings: 0.17 × 265 = 45L) — well BELOW the 150L target. Conditional on Priority-4-fires extracts for 87% of runs (savings: 230L) — well above target. The directive's intent (conditional loading to reduce context) is preserved; the specific signal is improved. Operator can override the reframing on review — the design is robust to either choice but the savings differ by 5x.

args.argument: PROP-040 names the new file 'decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md' but most of its content is integration mechanics, not queue mechanics. The naming is misleading; future tinker/decider runs reading this file will be confused about what's in it.
args.tinker_rebuttal: Real concern. Two options: (a) accept the operator-specified name and add a clarifying preamble inside the file (this PROP does both — see preamble_content_for_new_file); (b) rename to 'decider-curmudgeon-integration.md' or 'decider-expansion-integration.md'. PROP-040 defaults to (a) — operator's spec wins on naming, preamble clarifies internals. If operator prefers (b) on review, the rename is a 1-line edit (just the conditional-load instruction in decider.md). Surfaced for operator decision.

args.argument: PROP-034 / PROP-038 / PROP-039 will add more context elsewhere as their Phase 2s roll out. Net change may be neutral.
args.tinker_rebuttal: Exactly the point — without proactive extraction discipline paired with capability adds, every PROP that adds capability compounds context-load. Phase 1 of PROP-040 establishes the extraction pattern as a counter-balance. Future PROPs that add to decider-curmudgeon.md SHOULD consider whether the new content is conditional and extract proactively rather than retroactively. This PROP doesn't fix the compounding problem on its own — it's a structural change to allow future extractions to follow the same pattern.

args.argument: Why not just rollback PROP-037 instead of extracting?
args.tinker_rebuttal: PROP-037 closes a real bug — the integrity→decider intake gap that took 24h+ of operator labor to fix manually two days ago (per directive). Rollback recreates the failure mode. Operator-constraint #1 explicitly prohibits rollback of PROP-036/PROP-037/PROP-038. Extraction preserves the fix AND reduces context-load. Strictly dominant option vs rollback.

args.argument: Splitting decider-curmudgeon.md into two files creates a maintenance burden — future edits to integration mechanics might forget to update both files, or might land in the wrong file.
args.tinker_rebuttal: Real but bounded. Mitigations: (a) the two files have CLEAR domain separation (decider-curmudgeon.md = digest+yeet+closure+mode+cycle3; pq-mechanics.md = integration only); (b) cross-references are minimal (3 outbound from decider.md, 2 from routing-matrix.md, 1 from tinker-cost-engineering.md, 0 internal cross-references between the two split files); (c) tinker's claude_md_audit (CLAUDE.md Accuracy & Performance Audit) checks for File Ownership mismatches every Mode 3 — drift will be detected within the next tinker cycle. Phase 1 establishes the split; if maintenance burden materializes empirically, Phase 2 considerations might choose smaller extractions or different splits.

operator_specified_design_constraints_sa.no_rollback_of_prop_036_037_038: Confirmed. PROP-040 does NOT touch PROP-037's integrity intake severity-agnostic logic (which is the load-bearing fix per operator constraint #4). The split is text-relocation only.
operator_specified_design_constraints_sa.phase_1_decider_curmudgeon_md_only: Confirmed. No analyst/curmudgeon/tinker prompt edits in Phase 1. Phase 2 candidates surfaced but explicitly deferred.
operator_specified_design_constraints_sa.conditional_load_instruction_explicit_an: Confirmed. The IF/DO-NOT framing at decider.md Priority 4 makes the instruction visible at the dispatcher decision point. Not buried in a sub-reference.
operator_specified_design_constraints_sa.behavior_identical_to_pre_split: Confirmed. Move byte-for-byte. No content changes during the move. The conditional-load instruction in decider.md is the ONLY new prose authored in Phase 1; the moved content is verbatim.
operator_specified_design_constraints_sa.authorization_to_self_apply_after_operat: Confirmed (mirrors PROP-036/038 application pattern). After operator approval (HNOTE or directive update to 'approved'), tinker self-applies in a single decider-clone session.
operator_specified_design_constraints_sa.quantify_projected_savings_AND_re_valida: Confirmed. Projected: 230L avg/run, $7.55/year/agent. Re-validation cost: ~30-50 decider runs over 7 days × ~5 min/run operator-time-to-spot-check = ~3-4 hours total. Acceptable.

operator_action_requested.primary: Review this PROP. Decide on the new file name (operator's 'decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md' vs tinker's suggested 'decider-curmudgeon-integration.md'). Approve or amend.
operator_action_requested.secondary: Optionally confirm the Priority-4-fires reframing vs the directive's queue-depth framing. (Tinker's recommendation: Priority-4-fires, but the design is robust to either.)
operator_action_requested.on_approval: Tinker self-applies in the next tinker run via a decider-clone. Estimated apply-time: ~10 min. Phase 1 exit criteria measured at next Mode 3 (target: 2026-05-23, 7 days post-deployment).
operator_action_requested.on_amendment: Tinker revises PROP-040 per operator notes, re-submits as design-pending-operator-review (revision 2).
operator_action_requested.on_rejection: Mark directive completed with `superseded_reason: 'PROP-040 rejected by operator review — context-load growth continues to MONITOR threshold per FIND-08; future tinker runs may surface alternative extraction proposals.'`

implementation_plan_after_approval.step_1: In a clean clone (CLEAN_CLONE=/tmp/edit-clone), pull latest origin/main.
implementation_plan_after_approval.step_2: Read monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon.md lines 100-364 → write monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md (prepend preamble from preamble_content_for_new_file).
implementation_plan_after_approval.step_3: Edit monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon.md to remove lines 100-364 (keeping lines 1-99 and 365-end).
implementation_plan_after_approval.step_7: Edit monitor/prompts/reference/routing-matrix.md lines 8 and 21 per cross_reference_updates.routing_matrix_md_line_8.replacement and routing_matrix_md_line_21.replacement.
implementation_plan_after_approval.step_9: Verification: `diff <(git show HEAD:monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon.md) <(cat decider-curmudgeon.md decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md | sed -e 's/^# Decider: Expansion.*//' -e '/^\*\*This file is loaded ONLY/,/^For Step 2/d')` should produce only the preamble lines (post-extraction sum = pre-extraction). If diff fails, abort and investigate.
implementation_plan_after_approval.step_10: Run `node test.js` to ensure no test regressions (decider prompts don't have test coverage but build.js publish must still succeed).
implementation_plan_after_approval.step_11: Stage, commit, push: `git add monitor/prompts/decider.md monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon.md monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md monitor/prompts/reference/routing-matrix.md monitor/prompts/reference/tinker-cost-engineering.md && git commit -m 'PROP-040 Phase 1: extract Step 2a expansion integration to conditional reference' && git push origin main`.
implementation_plan_after_approval.step_12: Mark DIRECTIVE-20260516-002 as `status: 'completed'` with `completed_at`, `completed_by_run: <tinker-run-id>`, `prop_id_authored: 'PROP-040'`, and `amendment_file_authored: null` (this is an authoring directive, not an amendment).

primary: Empirical avg LOC savings on Priority-3-only and Priority-3b-only runs (target: ≥200L avg)
Priority-4-fires rate over the 7-day window (target: stable at ~13% ± 5%)
M2 and M3 ledger entries appearing on integration runs (target: no drop vs pre-split baseline)
Decider quality regression sample (5 patches reviewed by curmudgeon during Phase 1) — flags any quality-degradation
secondary: Tinker Mode 3 CLAUDE.md performance audit: decider context-load drops below baseline (target: ~2737L = pre-extraction 2967L minus ~230L avg)
Tinker's cumulative-growth signal: cross-agent context-load no longer trending up monotonically (PROP-040 Phase 2 candidates remain as future levers)

open_questions_for_operator: Q1 (filename): Stick with 'decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md' or rename to 'decider-curmudgeon-integration.md' / 'decider-expansion-integration.md'? Tinker's lean: rename to 'decider-curmudgeon-integration.md' for accuracy, but defers to operator.

Q2 (trigger framing): Phase 1 conditions on 'Priority 4 fires' (expansion-tracker has work). Original directive said 'priority-queue non-empty'. Tinker's lean: Priority-4-fires is more accurate AND gives ~5x more savings. Operator override possible.

Q3 (Phase 2 timing): If Phase 1 holds 7 green days, should tinker proceed with analyst.md or curmudgeon.md Phase 2 extraction? Both are candidates; tinker has no strong lean — depends on which agent's context-load growth concerns operator more.
